channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Amanda Holden shares her diet, fitness routine and approach to maintaining a healthy lifestyle at 55
item.description: Amanda Holden has spoken about the diet and exercise habits she says help her maintain her fitness and confidence at the age of 55. Speaking to the Daily Mail, the television presenter explained that ...

item.title: Lindsey Graham's Secret Meetings and Journalism Ethics
item.description: The article discusses a humorous exchange between MSNBC host Joe Scarborough and late Senator Lindsey Graham, highlighting Graham's tendency to involve the press in sensitive political matters. Scarbo...

item.title: Tanzania's Basketball League Receives Sh588.9m Sponsorship for Player Incentives
item.description: Basketball players in Tanzania’s Dar es Salaam League are set to receive significant financial rewards after sports betting company betPawa renewed its sponsorship deal for the 2026 season. The agreem...

item.title: Fishermen's Struggles Continue Despite Legal Victory in West Philippine Sea
item.description: Ten years after the 2016 arbitral ruling in the West Philippine Sea, Filipino fishermen continue to face harassment from Chinese vessels, despite the legal victory. Ryan Jacinto, a 43-year-old fisherm...

item.title: Shooting at Toronto Festival Leaves Two Dead, Several Injured
item.description: A shooting incident occurred at the Salsa on St. Clair festival in Toronto, resulting in two fatalities and multiple injuries. The event, which celebrated Latin culture, was attended by approximately ...

item.title: Mother Battles Stage Four Brain Cancer After Premature Birth
item.description: Amy Carroll, a 34-year-old mother from Northampton, faced a life-altering diagnosis during her fourth pregnancy. Initially experiencing dizziness and fainting, she was diagnosed with gestational diabe...

item.title: Australian universities to publish executive salaries and consultancy spending under new governance standards
item.description: Australian universities will be required to publish detailed financial information, including consultancy spending, executive salaries, and governance meeting decisions, under new federal standards. T...

item.title: Pakistan secures three bronze medals in 56th International Physics Olympiad
item.description: Pakistan has made significant strides in international science competitions by securing three bronze medals at the 56th International Physics Olympiad (IPhO) held in Bucaramanga, Colombia. The achieve...

item.title: Political clash as Umahi dismisses Obi's election fears
item.description: Minister of Works, David Umahi, has rejected claims by former Anambra State governor Peter Obi that his life is in danger ahead of the 2027 general election. Umahi stated that Obi would be alive to co...

item.title: AI and Quantum Computing Advance Drug Development by Creating New Peptides
item.description: A team from the Technical University of Denmark (DTU) and British startup ORCA Computing demonstrated how quantum computing can enhance generative AI models for drug discovery. By combining a quantum ...

item.title: The GOP's Cover-Up and Political Distrust in Modern America
item.description: The article critiques the Republican Party's alleged cover-ups and the erosion of public trust in political institutions. It highlights how figures like Donald Trump and Mitch McConnell have become sy...

item.title: U.S. Senator Lindsey Graham, Close Trump Ally, Dies at 71
item.description: Lindsey Graham, a U.S. Republican senator and close ally of former President Donald Trump, has died at the age of 71 following a brief illness. His office confirmed his passing on social media, statin...

item.title: Katie George: Sportscaster's Career, Marriage, and Achievements
item.description: Katie George is a renowned American sportscaster known for her versatility in covering multiple sports like college football, Formula 1, NBA, and volleyball. Born on December 3, 1993, in Louisville, K...

item.title: Nancy Mace Considers Running for Lindsey Graham's Senate Seat
item.description: The sudden death of South Carolina Senator Lindsey Graham has opened a Senate seat that Rep. Nancy Mace (R-SC) is reportedly considering for a potential run. A source close to Mace told The Hill repor...

item.title: Manmohan Singh's Response to Election Commission Criticism During 2012 Elections

item.title: Congress Aims to Shift Ayodhya Narrative Amid Temple Donation Controversy

item.title: Canadian Challenges: Housing, Bureaucracy, and Public Dissatisfaction
item.description: The article compiles reader letters criticizing Canada's political and social issues. Contributors discuss Prime Minister Mark Carney's housing policies, including plans to address the 24 Sussex Drive...

item.title: Assassin's Creed Black Flag Remake Review: A Nostalgic Update with Modern Graphics
item.description: Ubisoft's *Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced* has been released 13 years after its original 2013 launch, leveraging advancements in graphics and gameplay. The remake, set in the Caribbean during th...

item.title: S. Janaki's enduring legacy in Kerala through music and family bonds
item.description: The article chronicles the profound impact of singer S. Janaki on Abhilash Pudukad's family in Kerala, highlighting how her music became an integral part of their lives. Abhilash, who grew up surround...

item.title: AI's Role in Transforming South Africa's Higher Education System
item.description: The article explores how artificial intelligence is poised to revolutionize higher education in South Africa by addressing systemic challenges such as the skills gap and the temporal mismatch between ...
